claim_text: The patient has lower back pain.
The patient injured their back while moving boxes.
The patient's back pain persisted for several months.
The patient attempted PT at the gym.
The patient's back pain is still present.
The patient had knee surgery two years ago.
The patient denies fever.
The patient denies chills.
The patient denies incontinence.
The patient denies bowel problems.
The patient is taking Tylenol.
The patient is taking Ibuprofen.
The patient's temperature is 98.6F.
The patient's blood pressure is 170/96.
The patient's heart rate is 68 bpm.
The patient's oxygenation is 95% on room air.
The patient has a bony protuberance of C5/C6.
The patient has decreased flexion and extension of the lower back.
The patient has a positive straight leg raise.
The patient has spasm of the paraspinal muscles of the lumbar spine.
The patient has acute disk herniation.
The patient may have arthritis in the neck.
The patient has pain in the lower back.
The patient has muscle spasm in the lumbar spine.
The patient has decreased range of motion in the lower back.
The patient will have a lumbar spine MRI.
The patient will be referred to physical therapy.
The patient will take Meloxicam 15mg once daily.
The patient will follow up with the physician in 2 weeks for MRI review and plan.
The patient will return to physical therapy.
The patient may need an epidural steroid injection.
